result.stdout:
Generates database access logic based on provided SQL queries
USAGE: npgsql-generator [--help] [--version] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    generate <options>    Generate files
    create-command <options>
                          Add a new command template to a repository template
                          file
    create-repository <options>
                          Create an empty repository template file

    Use 'npgsql-generator <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --version, -v         Print version and exit
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
Generates database access logic based on provided SQL queries
USAGE: npgsql-generator create-command [--help] [--prepared] [--single-row]
                                       --repository <path> <name>

NAME:

    <name>                Name of the command

OPTIONS:

    --prepared, -p        Whether the generator should generate a prepared
                          statement
    --single-row, -sr     Execution of the command is supposed to return 0 or 1
                          rows
    --repository, -r <path>
                          Path of the repository descriptor to append to
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
Generates database access logic based on provided SQL queries
USAGE: npgsql-generator create-repository [--help] [--namespace <namespace>]
                                          [--output <path>] <name>

REPOSITORY NAME:

    <name>                Name of the repository

OPTIONS:

    --namespace, -n <namespace>
                          .NET namespace of the repository
    --output, -o <path>   Directory for the generated output
    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
Generates database access logic based on provided SQL queries
USAGE: npgsql-generator generate [--help] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    all <options>         Generate both user defined types and repositories
    types <options>       Generate user defined types only
    repositories <options>
                          Generate repositories only

    Use 'npgsql-generator <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --help                display this list of options.

commands.payload:
Generates database access logic based on provided SQL queries
USAGE: npgsql-generator generate [--help] [<subcommand> [<options>]]

SUBCOMMANDS:

    all <options>         Generate both user defined types and repositories
    types <options>       Generate user defined types only
    repositories <options>
                          Generate repositories only

    Use 'npgsql-generator <subcommand> --help' for additional information.

OPTIONS:

    --help                display this list of options.

result.stdout:
Generates database access logic based on provided SQL queries
USAGE: npgsql-generator generate all [--help] --connection-string <connstring>
                                     [--udf-namespace <namespace>]
                                     [--output-path <path>]
                                     [--top-level-connections]
                                     [--record-return-types] <files>...

FILES:

    <files>...            Input files to process

OPTIONS:

    --connection-string, -c <connstring>
                          Conn string to connect to dev postgres instance
    --udf-namespace, -ns <namespace>
                          Namespace for user defined types [Default=Db.Types]
    --output-path, -o <path>
                          Directory to place the generated files
    --top-level-connections, -tlc
                          Accept NpgsqlConnections at repository level instead
                          of command level [default=false]
    --record-return-types, -rrt
                          Generate classic, non anonymous F# record types as
                          return types. Non anonymous result types help in
                          better iterop with C# [default=true]
    --help                display this list of options.

                                       [--udf-namespace <namespace>]
                                       [--output-path <path>]
